sections.paragraph:
ἐπισφραγίζω (s. σφραγίζω) 1 aor. ἐπεσφράγισα (Diod S 14, 55, 1 βιβλίον ἐπεσφραγισμένον; pap; 2 Esdr 20:1; TestSol 7:8 ἐπεσφράγισα; the mid. in Pla. et al.; Philo and Mel. P. 57, 415) to put a seal on someth., seal, τ. διωγμόν the persecution, thereby bringing it to an end, as a letter MPol 1:1.—DELG s.v. σφραγίς.

aor. aor. = aorist

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Mel. Mel. = Melito of Sardis, II a.d.—List 5

someth. someth. = something

MPol MPol = Martyrdom of Polycarp; after II a.d.—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 383
